input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
                <!-- wrapping trick not needed; see below -->
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

The cleaner pattern is to **wrap the entire CollectionView** in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                    SelectionChangedCommandParameter="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using MVVM Community Toolkit):
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool isRefreshing;

    // Fires on pull-to-refresh
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
    }

    // Fires when selection changes
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelectedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;    // reset so user can re-select
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"` | Syncs selection to VM; set `null` to deselect |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Triggers navigation on tap |
| `IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"` | Controls the refresh spinner; set `false` when done |
| `Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"` | Calls your reload logic on pull-down |

**Important notes:**
- `SelectionMode="Single"` — only one item selectable at a time.
- Reset `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the same item can be tapped again.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` at the end of your refresh method or the spinner won't stop.
- `RefreshView` must wrap `CollectionView` — not the other way around.
